from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about a CelestialBody into a fluent sentence.
    If all triples share the same subject, produce a single complex sentence that
    lists the predicates without repeating the subject. Otherwise fall back to the
    original clause‑by‑clause approach.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Templates that start after the subject (no leading subject token)
    pred_templates = {
        "apoapsis": "has an apoapsis of {object}",
        "periapsis": "has a periapsis of {object}",
        "orbitalPeriod": "has an orbital period of {object}",
        "epoch": "was recorded at epoch {object}",
        "discoverer": "was discovered by {object}",
        "discovered": "was discovered on {object}",
        "averageSpeed": "moves at an average speed of {object}",
        "escapeVelocity": "has an escape velocity of {object}",
        "mass": "has a mass of {object}",
        "temperature": "has a temperature of {object}",
        "density": "has a density of {object}",
        "rotationPeriod": "has a rotation period of {object}",
        "maximumTemperature": "reaches a maximum temperature of {object}",
        "meanTemperature": "has a mean temperature of {object}",
        "minimumTemperature": "reaches a minimum temperature of {object}",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as {object}",
        "absoluteMagnitude": "has an absolute magnitude of {object}",
        "surfaceArea": "has a surface area of {object}",
        "deathCause": "died of {object}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {object}",
        "nationality": "is {object}",
        "stateOfOrigin": "originates from {object}",
        "almaMater": "studied at {object}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {object}",
        "doctoralStudent": "supervised doctoral student {object}",
        "deathDate": "died on {object}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {object}",
    }

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Determine if all subjects are identical
    first_subject = triples[0].subject
    same_subject = all(t.subject == first_subject for t in triples)

    if same_subject:
        # Build predicate phrases without repeating the subject
        phrases = []
        for t in triples:
            tmpl = pred_templates.get(t.predicate, "{predicate} {object}")
            phrase = tmpl.format(predicate=t.predicate, object=clean_obj(t.object))
            phrases.append(phrase)
        # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last item
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            body = phrases[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
        sentence = f"{first_subject} {body}."
    else:
        # Fallback: repeat subject for each clause (original behaviour)
        clauses = []
        for t in triples:
            tmpl = pred_templates.get(t.predicate, "{predicate} {object}")
            clause = f"{t.subject} {tmpl.format(predicate=t.predicate, object=clean_obj(t.object))}"
            clauses.append(clause)
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentence = clauses[0] + "."
        else:
            sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: CelestialBody. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about a CelestialBody into a fluent sentence.
    Handles multiple triples by joining clause fragments with commas and a final
    conjunction.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple template map for known predicates; fall back to a generic pattern.
    # Templates avoid adding units that are already present in the object string.
    templates = {
        "apoapsis": "{subject} has an apoapsis of {object}",
        "periapsis": "{subject} has a periapsis of {object}",
        "orbitalPeriod": "{subject} has an orbital period of {object}",
        "epoch": "{subject} was recorded at epoch {object}",
        "discoverer": "{subject} was discovered by {object}",
        "discovered": "{subject} was discovered on {object}",
        "averageSpeed": "{subject} moves at an average speed of {object}",
        "escapeVelocity": "{subject} has an escape velocity of {object}",
        "mass": "{subject} has a mass of {object}",
        "temperature": "{subject} has a temperature of {object}",
        "density": "{subject} has a density of {object}",
        "rotationPeriod": "{subject} has a rotation period of {object}",
        "maximumTemperature": "{subject} reaches a maximum temperature of {object}",
        "meanTemperature": "{subject} has a mean temperature of {object}",
        "minimumTemperature": "{subject} reaches a minimum temperature of {object}",
        "formerName": "{subject} was formerly known as {object}",
        "absoluteMagnitude": "{subject} has an absolute magnitude of {object}",
        "surfaceArea": "{subject} has a surface area of {object}",
        "deathCause": "{subject} died of {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} is {object}",
        "stateOfOrigin": "{subject} originates from {object}",
        "almaMater": "{subject} studied at {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "doctoralStudent": "{subject} supervised doctoral student {object}",
        "deathDate": "{subject} died on {object}",
        "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate,
                             "{subject} {predicate} {object}")
        clause = tmpl.format(subject=triple.subject,
                             predicate=triple.predicate,
                             object=triple.object)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
